profiles.title: Kalra 2022 microtubule transport with Zhang 2017 discrete time-crystal benchmark
notes: Microtubule transport length is source-backed from Kalra et al. (6.64 nm) and the diffusion coefficient is source-backed from the same paper (3.15e-5 cm^2 s^-1).
The transport-timescale proxy is inferred as L^2/(4D) using the reported 2D diffusion length and diffusion coefficient; it is not a direct biological decoherence measurement.

The time-crystal channel is source-backed only at the benchmark level: period-doubled subharmonic response implies subharmonic_lock_ratio = 2 and time_crystal_signature_pass = true for the discrete-time-crystal benchmark papers.

profiles.title: Kalra 2022 etomidate-dampened microtubule transport with Bruno 2015 conservative no-go contrast
notes: The perturbed microtubule contrast uses the etomidate-dampened diffusion length reported by Kalra et al. (5.6 nm at 50 uM etomidate).

The replay retains the baseline source-backed diffusion coefficient from the same paper as a reference coefficient for the reduced-order transport-lifetime proxy because the packet does not extract a separate anesthetic-specific D from the main-text source.

The time-crystal channel is intentionally conservative here: Bruno's no-go boundary is encoded as subharmonic_lock_ratio = 1 and time_crystal_signature_pass = false, yielding a sourced negative contrast rather than a biological time-crystal claim.
